name: Coles & Holton, LLP
description_short: Nashville-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings. Joshua A. Coles offers personalized representation across Middle Tennessee counties.
description_long:
Coles & Holton, LLP is a bankruptcy law firm headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee, founded by attorney Joshua A. Coles. The firm operates with a philosophy that bankruptcy represents a legal right to debt relief and a fresh start, rather than financial failure. They serve the Greater Nashville and Middle Tennessee region, including Davidson County, Montgomery County, and Robertson County, with coverage extending to cities like Clarksville, Springfield, Brentwood, and Hendersonville.

The firm specializes in both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings. Chapter 7 services focus on eliminating unsecured debts (credit cards, payday loans, medical bills) while potentially preserving assets like homes and vehicles. Chapter 13 services help individuals with steady income restructure debts into manageable repayment plans over up to 5 years, with emphasis on preventing home foreclosure and protecting asset equity. They also address specific concerns like tax refund treatment in bankruptcy proceedings and provide foreclosure prevention consultations.

Coles & Holton distinguishes itself through emphasis on personalized, one-on-one representation without judgment or intimidation. The firm explicitly states they "take the time to personally get to know each of our clients" and offer the representation "people expect and deserve." Customer reviews consistently highlight professionalism, clarity of explanation, and pain-free handling of complex situations. The firm positions itself as accessible legal counsel for individuals facing involuntary financial hardship (job loss, accidents, medical emergencies) rather than the result of irresponsibility.

The firm's primary limitation is geographic scope—services are restricted to Middle Tennessee and surrounding counties. As a solo or small practice centered on one attorney (Joshua Coles), availability and responsiveness may vary. The website lacks detailed pricing information, attorney credentials, or experience metrics, requiring direct consultation to evaluate fit. Bankruptcy itself involves multi-month processes and significant life impact, making this service appropriate only for those genuinely needing debt discharge or restructuring.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Offers both Chapter 7 (debt elimination) and Chapter 13 (restructuring) options tailored to different income situations
Personalized one-on-one representation rather than mass-production legal services
Serves multi-county region including Nashville, Clarksville, Springfield, and surrounding areas
Addresses specific concerns like tax refund protection and foreclosure prevention
Client reviews emphasize clear explanation of complex bankruptcy processes
Free consultation available to evaluate eligibility and options
Employer notification is avoided during Chapter 7 filing process
cons: No detailed pricing or fee structure provided on website—requires direct contact for cost transparency
Limited to Middle Tennessee geography; not accessible to out-of-state clients
No information on attorney credentials, years of experience, or case success rates
Small practice centered on single attorney may limit availability or timeline for consultation
Bankruptcy process takes 5 months (Chapter 7) to 5 years (Chapter 13)—not a quick solution
best_for: Middle Tennessee residents facing unsecured debt (credit cards, medical bills, payday loans) seeking Chapter 7 discharge
Homeowners in danger of foreclosure seeking Chapter 13 restructuring to preserve equity
Individuals with involuntary financial hardship (job loss, medical emergency, accident) needing legal debt relief guidance
Those preferring local, personalized legal representation over large bankruptcy mills
diagnosis: Coles & Holton is appropriate for Middle Tennessee residents needing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy representation, particularly those facing foreclosure or involuntary financial hardship. The primary caveat is that bankruptcy is a lengthy legal process (5 months to 5 years) requiring commitment and honest financial disclosure—it is not a quick fix and should only be pursued after other options are exhausted or deemed unsuitable.
services: Unsecured debt elimination (credit cards, medical bills, payday loans)
Multi-county legal representation (Davidson, Montgomery, Robertson counties)
Debt repayment plan development and negotiation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Nashville-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings across Middle Tennessee.
